entry.title: 【Day 3】冰岛五日游
entry.content:
第三天去黄金圈啦~

公交站路上

早上要去公交站和导演汇合。走过去的路上看到了可爱的猫猫~

entry.title: 【Day 2】冰岛五日游
entry.content:
在冰岛的第二天~

早餐

在Brauð & Co买了肉桂卷和香草卷~
这家店非常出名，有评论说是世界上最好吃的肉桂面包（

香草卷和肉桂卷都是830冰岛克朗一个，新鲜出炉的~个人没有很喜欢肉桂所以选了香草味的。感觉刚开始吃的几口还不错，稍微有点甜（吃不惯甜食）
吃到一半就感觉太甜太腻了。朋友对肉桂卷的评价也是如此。

街边

街上的路标很神奇呢~还以为是不准牵小孩（） 翻译后发现这是步行街的意思。

这两天天气不太好，不是在下小雨就是阴天。整个城市都被乌云笼罩着，心情也阴郁了起来qwq
还有就是根本无法从天空分辨时间哇！无论早上中午或是晚上都是一个样子。。。

跳蚤市场

还是去逛逛市中心的跳蚤市场吧~ 这个跳蚤市场叫Kolaportið，只在周末营业。

导游说冰岛和日本开始捕食鲸鱼都是米国爸爸怂恿的（

冰岛有很多马噢

冰岛传统毛衣（Lopapeysa）是由冰岛羊的未纺羊毛制成，肩部有独特的圆形花纹。这里卖的不便宜，一件要150欧左右。

大叔很热情的给我们展示了鱼皮领带和各种各样的干鱼皮，包括鲨鱼皮和三文鱼皮。

冰岛阴茎博物馆

去跳蚤市场对面的阴茎博物馆啦~ 是世界上展出最多阴茎的博物馆噢~ 票价3500冰岛克朗，并没有学生票qwq

博物馆里面有好多动物的阴茎标本。介绍里还有动物的交配习惯和阴茎大小等，非常详细。

海边

从阴茎博物馆出来后就到附近的海边转转

市中心闲逛

从海边逛到市中心和市政府大楼附近啦~

彩虹街

逛逛这边的店~

晚饭

晚饭去了 Icelandic Street Food，主卖特色汤配面包。一碗汤+面包是2500冰岛克朗，如果用特色面包碗盛汤的话就是3000冰岛克朗。汤可以无限续噢！

三种汤随便续，我们点了羊肉汤和海鲜汤（Shellfish）。建议到店第一次点先点海鲜汤，店员会捞很多小龙虾，之后就续羊肉汤。如果要续海鲜汤的话就没有几个虾了，全是水qwq
羊肉汤怎么续都会给足了料，所以比较划算。海鲜汤有股奶油味，很神奇~ 羊肉汤里面有大块的羊肉、土豆和胡萝卜，很好喝！（我干了五碗）
在一个热狗都要卖830的冰岛，这家店性价比太高了！强力推荐！！！

饭后闲逛

哈尔格林姆教堂

教堂入口处有个超大的管风琴，我们进去的时候有人在演奏。感觉这个乐器特别难练，需要手脚并用。

entry.title: 【Day 1】冰岛五日游
entry.content:
去阿冰家玩啦~

阿姆斯特丹史基浦机场

本来没觉得新加坡樟宜机场有多好。。。直到看到了阿姆斯特丹史基浦机场。机场很大，但有点老旧且缺乏引导，几个区域分不太清。过道太窄，电瓶车开过来的时候占满了整个过道，行人必须停下脚步靠边站才能让出位置给电瓶车开过去。
洗手间又小又旧，有扇门还坏掉了。

机场有在卖郁金香种子，品种还不少。一包10欧左右。

雷克雅未克凯夫拉维克机场（Keflavík International Airport）

从阿姆斯特丹飞往冰岛雷克雅未克（Reykjavík）需要大约3小时20分钟。落地后不用过海关，也不用检查签证/身份证/护照（盖章啥的），蛮意外的。凯夫拉维克机场不大，但是装修现代、洗手间也非常干净。有意思的是走向机场出口必须穿过一片购物区，有点景区纪念品店内味了（）

预订了FlyBus从机场前往市中心~

地上黄黄绿绿的并不是草，而是百年老青苔（

公路上很多地方都在维修，给人一种基础建设不太好，在乡下的感觉（）

雷克雅未克市

刚下车没走两步就看到猫猫了！！毛茸茸的超可爱！！！！

谁能想到这张照片的拍摄时间是晚上9点哇~ 8月的日落很晚呢~

这条街走下去就是大海，海的对面是。。。（不是敌人！）海的对面是山啦~ 是埃夏山（Mt. Esja）

购物街一直走上去就是冰岛最出名的教堂：哈尔格林姆教堂（Hallgrimskirkja）

身后就是著名的彩虹购物街啦（游客街）

即使是晚上9点多了人也不少呢

晚餐

尝了比尔·克林顿吃过的热狗 Bæjarins Beztu Pylsur
据说是全欧洲最好吃的热狗~

买的时候外面下着小雨，站在屋檐下吃完的。。。天气比较冷，热热的热狗很不错！里面还有炸洋葱碎。黄色的是芥末酱，棕色的是咸咸的酱汁（具体是什么吃不太出来）。听说热狗是羊肉做的噢！味道确实好，但价格有点小贵，830冰岛克朗一个。朋友评价说不如宜家热狗qwq（后来在Bonus超市里找到了同款热狗，五个600冰岛克朗左右。。。之后就自己做了）

饭后闲逛

回去的路上彩虹街上人很少，拍到了不错的照片~

后来又走到哈尔格林姆教堂附近拍了两张

entry.title: Visiting Foodhallen
entry.content:
Fourth day~

Lunch

Boiled a pack of pork and chive frozen dumplings. On sale it was about €3 a pack. Taste was just… okay…

On the way out I saw the dorm cat! Its name is Simba~ so adorable~ ❤
Heard from my Dutch friend that Dutch people always call orange cats Simba, because they look like the lion Simba XD

Dinner

For dinner we were planning to check out Amsterdam’s food court, Foodhallen. Google reviews looked pretty good.

There are tons of stalls inside: crepes, sushi, Vietnamese spring rolls, ramen, burgers, burritos, etc.

I ordered Bitterballen from De Ballen Bar. It is a traditional Dutch fried meatball. 4 for €4.25.

The sauce taste a bit weird and there is mustard inside. Don’t really enjoy it personally :P

The meatballs were beef. Inside they were soft and a bit loose, so at first bite I thought they were undercooked (but that’s actually how those meatballs are). The filling had some spices (couldn’t tell exactly which) and I didn’t find them especially tasty.

I also ordered a burger from The Butcher. The patty was grilled over charcoal. It was fine, a little bland. Price was about €13.

Stroll after dinner

Saw some nice buildings on the way back.

Also saw a super cute cat in a residential area!!! It came up and rubbed both our legs!!! Loved it!!!!








Duck with dark eye circles next to the canal XD

And there were people kayaking in the canal.

entry.title: Continue exploring the city centre
entry.content:
Third day in Amsterdam~

Lunch

Reheated last night’s leftovers in the microwave and ate them with white bread and pickles.

Dinner

For dinner, we went to a highly rated Dutch restaurant. When I was looking up tips online a lot of travel blogs mentioned this place, really eager to try it~

The decor was really special: photos of mothers everywhere~

We ordered the Hollandse Rijsttafel / Dutch rice dish — basically a little bit of each classic dish.
There were three dishes in total: Stamppot (hotchpotch), Suddervlees, and Hachée. The latter two were both stewed beef.
I’m not sure what the spread on the bread was made from, but it was delicious!

The two stewed-beef dishes tasted similar, one had slightly smaller chunks of beef and was cooked until more fall-apart tender. The apple sauce was pretty sweet, with large chunks of apple. The pickled purple radish was sweet and delicious. There were roast potatoes, steamed potatoes, and mashed potatoes mixed with sauerkraut. As expected, I’m still not a big fan of sauerkraut ().

I heard the sausages were from HEMA. I can buy some and try making it myself later. My personal score: 7/10!

Strolling in the city

Very cute Mini cars that can go onto the bicycle paths

Really nice weather, took a few photos of the canal~

We passed by a gelato store and saw very interesting flavours!

I later asked a local Dutch friend, apparently “Smurf” flavor is quite popular in the Netherlands and kids especially love it. But it seems to be just a plain sweet flavor?

entry.title: Amsterdam Chinatown
entry.content:
A visit to the city center~

Albert Heijn

There are a few supermarkets next to my dorm. Found some very interesting stuff:

Chinatown

Went to the Chinatown in city centre and browsed a few Chinese supermarkets. The products are actually cheaper than regular supermarkets in Amsterdam.

Saw many interesting architecture. There is actually a Chinese temple right in the centre of the street!

Dinner

We ate our dinner at a Chinese restaurant in Chinatown. The vegetarian and meat dishes have similar prices, around €20.

Here, every dish ordered comes with a free bowl of rice… kind of weird.

The packaging even has a tutorial on how to use chopsticks! First time seeing that~

The three of us ordered two dishes: one vegetarian, one meat. The portions were pretty large. The veg was luohan zhai (Buddha’s delight), which seemed to have about 7–8 ingredients: bean sprouts, bamboo shoots, broccoli, stuff like that. The meat was a three-roast combo. We had some left overs and packed it to go. Personally it was pretty meh… I’m sure I’d cook better myself XD.
Score: 4.5/10…

Finally I took a few photos in the city center~ They came out quite nice~

entry.title: Reach Amsterdam
entry.content:
Reached the Netherlands~

Hostel

My dorm is right next to campus. The location’s pretty good, only a five-minute walk to the train station~
There’s also a supermarket nearby, just a five-minute bike ride away!

Bicycles

As soon as I got off the train I saw so many bicycles… Sasuga kingdom of bikes~

There’s a bike parking area next to the university library, packed wall-to-wall with bikes… It’s my first time seeing so many bicycles!

Dinner

The restaurant was recommended by a friend. It is a highly reviewes Japanese ramen restaurant in the city center.

I ordered the daily special signature ramen (forgot the exact name), and since the description said it was extremely spicy I went for it (). The price was about €18.70.

It’s a tonkotsu broth with red chili oil on top. I suspect they added Pixian doubanjiang (spicy fermented bean paste)… because I actually bit into a few of those soybeans you get in the bean paste, and the flavor was pretty similar. The noodles are Sapporo-style curly yellow noodles, topped with roasted chicken, a huge handful of coriander, pickled bamboo shoots, fried onions, a few stalks of bok choy and chunks of napa cabbage.

The bean paste, coriander, bok choy and napa cabbage give me this stitched-together Sino-Japanese vibe (?), which is kind of weird. And it wasn’t as spicy as I imagined.

My friend ordered a bowl of miso ramen, and halfway through thought it was insanely salty…

Considering that price, you could eat 1.5 bowls of ramen in Singapore or 3 bowls in Japan… so I’ll give it a 6/10…

The moment I stepped out of the ramen shop I smelled a really weird foul odor and realized the place next door was that legendary “coffee shop” (weed shop).
(I hightailed it.)

entry.title: Flying Over
entry.content:
Singapore –> Amsterdam

Flying with KLM (Royal Dutch Airlines).
I originally wanted to pick Qatar or Emirates — comfy and cheap. But when I was booking, Israel and Iran were causing trouble again… Singapore Airlines even cancelled flights to Dubai… so I went with the direct flight after all.
The flight time is great: it leaves Singapore at midnight and lands in the Netherlands 13 hours later in the morning (so I could sleep the whole way).
It sounded lovely in my head, but the plane isn’t that comfortable and I still couldn’t fall asleep, qwq
That’s life for a broke student…

Boarding

KLM’s signature color is blue~ I only learned from the description that it comes from Delft pottery produced in the Netherlands. But it kind of looks like Chinese blue-and-white porcelain…

I looked it up and it really is modeled on traditional Chinese porcelain…

Airplane meal

Dinner


For the first meal I could choose between fried rice + satay chicken or beef-and-tomato pasta. I’ll definitely go with the first one hehes.
The fried rice tasted okay — it even had tiny dried shrimp. But the portion was way too small QAQ.
The side was sweet-and-sour shredded radish mixed with rice vermicelli (?), kind of strange.
Dessert was a blueberry jam cake, a bit too sweet qwq

Breakfast


The second airplane meal was breakfast — there were no options this time. It was mini pancakes + a classic roll + fruit + yogurt.
The pancakes with the cream spread was really tasty, not too sweet~

Others

Saw the sunrise from the plane yeyyeyy!! I think it was when we were flying over Turkey! (I wasn’t in the window seat so my photos turned out awful, sorryz)
